title: 'Y' Are The Maiden Posies
content:
"'Y' are the maiden posies,
        And so graced,
        To be placed
        Fore damask roses.
        Yet, though thus respected,
        By and by
        Ye do lie,
        Poor girls, neglected.'
meaning:
Louisa May Alcott's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "'Y' Are The Maiden Posies"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
